title: AVA Run Manifest
description: Complete descriptor for one simulation run (RTL, ISS, formal, or compliance). Written by the orchestrator before the run starts; updated in-place as each agent completes its phase. Serves as the single source of truth for run configuration, provenance, status, outputs, and diagnostics. Schema version 2.0.0.

hex_size.description: Hex-encoded size or offset value. No minimum digit count — can represent any positive integer.

run_id.description: Globally unique run identifier. Recommended format: '{ISO8601_compact}_{seed}_{8hex}', e.g. '20240315T142300Z_42_ab3f9c01'.

iso8601_utc.description: ISO-8601 timestamp in UTC with Z suffix. Sub-second precision optional.

sha256.description: SHA-256 hex digest (lowercase, 64 chars). Used for binary and source integrity.

lifecycle_status.description: Granular lifecycle state. Orchestrator and agents update this field atomically (file-lock required). Terminal states: passed, failed, timeout, build_error, infra_error, cancelled.

error_msg.description: First 2 KB of error output if non-zero exit.

log_path.description: Path to full stdout/stderr log relative to run_dir.

type.description: ram=read/write DRAM; rom=read-only; mmio=memory-mapped I/O (no bus wait model); tcm=tightly-coupled memory; iomem=I/O with side-effects.

writable.description: Overrides type default. Explicit false on a ram region marks it write-protected (e.g. stack guard page). Default: true for ram/tcm, false for rom.

executable.description: Whether instructions may be fetched from this region. Default: true for ram/rom/tcm, false for mmio/iomem.

cacheable.description: Hint for pipeline/cache model. Default: true for ram/rom/tcm.

init_file.description: Optional path to a $readmemh-compatible hex file to pre-load this region. Relative to run_dir.

mmio_model.description: Behavioral MMIO model to instantiate for this region (RTL harness only).

parent_run_id.description: If this run was spawned by the coverage director or red-team agent as a targeted follow-up, this field links back to the parent run. Null for root runs.

campaign_id.description: Groups related runs into a named regression campaign for analytics aggregation, e.g. 'nightly_m_ext_2024Q1'. Null for ad-hoc runs.

run_type.description: Primary purpose of this run. 'differential'=RTL vs ISS compare; 'compliance'=RISCOF sig compare; 'formal'=model-checking pass; 'coverage_directed'=driven by cold-path feedback; 'adversarial'=red-team generated; 'smoke'=quick sanity.

isa.description: Complete ISA configuration. Both DUT and ISS are configured from these fields by the orchestrator — never configure them independently.

xlen.description: Register width. Schema v2 validates for 32; 64 is accepted but triggers a warning from the orchestrator.

items.description: Each element MUST be one of the listed enum values. Any string not in this list (e.g. 'Q', 'X', 'rv32g') is a schema error. Add new extensions here AND bump schema version.

extensions.description: Enabled ISA extensions. 'I' is mandatory. Phase 1 support: I, M, Zicsr, Zifencei. Remaining extensions are reserved for Phase 2+. CRITICAL: items.enum is the whitelist — unlisted extensions are REJECTED.

priv.description: Enabled privilege modes. M is mandatory. ['M'] for v2 M-only; ['M','S','U'] for future full privilege.

isa_string.description: Canonical ISA string passed verbatim to ISS and Verilator, e.g. 'rv32im_zicsr_zifencei'. Derived by orchestrator from `xlen` + `extensions`; do not set manually.

endianness.description: Byte order. RISC-V is little-endian by default; big-endian is legal but rare.

seed.description: 32-bit RNG seed passed to test generator, RTL simulator, and ISS. Must produce bit-identical output for any fixed (seed, binary) pair. The orchestrator rejects seeds outside [0, 2^32-1].

path.description: Path to test binary relative to run_dir.

format.description: 'elf32' is the canonical format. 'hex' and 'binary' are accepted for bare-metal injection flows.

entry.description: Reset / entry PC. Typically 0x80000000 for RISC-V bare-metal. Must match the ELF entry point if format=elf32.

sha256.description: SHA-256 digest of the binary file. Agents verify this before use. Orchestrator computes it at manifest creation time. A mismatch aborts the run with infra_error.

size_bytes.description: File size in bytes. Cross-checked against disk at run start.

generator.description: Provenance of the binary if machine-generated.

dut.description: RTL DUT simulator configuration. All fields read by Agent B.

tool.description: RTL simulation backend. 'verilator' is the primary supported backend with full coverage API.

rtl_sources.description: Ordered list of RTL source files relative to repo root. Must include the top-level module first. Agent B derives the Verilator compile command from this list.

rtl_source_hashes.description: Map from RTL source path to SHA-256 hash. Populated by orchestrator. Enables cache invalidation on RTL change.

verilator_flags.description: Extra Verilator CLI flags appended after standard AVA flags. Example: ['--Wall', '--Wno-UNUSED', '--assert']. Do NOT include coverage flags here — they are controlled by coverage_config.

timeout_cycles.description: Maximum simulated clock cycles before the harness halts and reports 'timeout'. Must be large enough for the test binary to complete. Rule of thumb: set to 10x the expected instruction count.

timeout_wall_s.description: Maximum wall-clock seconds before the OS process is killed. Provides a safety net if the simulator hangs in an infinite loop that somehow evades cycle counting.

coverage_config.description: Verilator coverage knobs. Absent or null disables coverage collection.

annotate_dir.description: Directory for annotated source files (verilator_coverage --annotate). Null to skip.

waveform_config.description: Waveform dump settings. Null disables waveform collection (default for CI — saves disk).

start_cycle.description: Cycle to start dumping. Null = from start.

log_loads.description: Whether to emit mem_reads entries. Increases log size by ~30%.

flush_every.description: Flush to disk every N records to bound data loss on crash.

plusargs.description: Verilog +arg=value pairs injected into simulation, e.g. {"TIMEOUT": "100000"}.

build_cache_key.description: Hash of (rtl_sources, defines, verilator_flags) used for build caching. Computed by orchestrator.

iss.description: ISS (Instruction Set Simulator) golden reference configuration. All fields read by Agent C.

tool.description: 'spike' is the primary reference ISS. 'sail-riscv' for theorem-prover-level cross-check. 'none' for formal-only or compliance-only runs.

isa_string.description: ISA string passed to ISS CLI. Derived by orchestrator from isa.isa_string — do not set independently.

extra_flags.description: Additional ISS CLI flags, e.g. ['--log-commits', '--misaligned']. Do NOT include the binary path or ISA string — orchestrator adds those.

memory_flags.description: Memory region flags derived from memory_map and passed to ISS, e.g. ['-m0x80000000:0x10000']. Orchestrator auto-generates these from the memory_map field — set manually only to override.

spike_log_flag.description: Flag to enable commit logging in Spike. '--log-commits' for recent Spike; '--enable-commitlog' for older builds.

timeout_wall_s.description: ISS wall-clock timeout. Should be significantly lower than RTL timeout_cycles wall equivalent.

formal.description: Formal verification configuration. Non-null only when run_type='formal'. Null for all other run types.

cex_file.description: Counterexample witness file path relative to run_dir.

compliance.description: RISCOF / riscv-arch-test compliance configuration. Non-null when run_type='compliance'.

suite_path.description: Path to arch test suite root relative to repo root.

dut_plugin.description: Path to RISCOF DUT plugin Python file.

ref_plugin.description: Path to RISCOF reference (Spike) plugin Python file.

test_list.description: Explicit list of test names to run. Null means run the full suite.

signature_dir.description: Directory for DUT signature outputs relative to run_dir.

ref_sig_dir.description: Directory for golden (reference ISS) signature files.

memory_map.description: Physical memory map. Both DUT and ISS are configured from this field by the orchestrator. At least one RAM region must be present and must contain the binary entry point.

run_dir.description: Absolute path of the run working directory. All relative paths in `outputs`, `binary.path`, `dut.*_output`, and `iss.*_output` resolve from this directory. Must be writable before run start.

status.description: Current lifecycle state. See $defs.lifecycle_status for valid transitions.

phases.description: Per-agent phase tracking. Each key is updated by the owning agent upon phase start/end.

outputs.description: Populated by agents as they complete. All paths are relative to run_dir. Null means the output was not produced (phase skipped or not yet run).

bug_report.description: JSON bug report if mismatch (Agent D). Null on pass.

signature_dir.description: Directory of DUT signature files (Agent E).

formal_results.description: Formal tool output directory (formal run only).

total_instrs.description: Total instructions retired (from ISS commit log record count).

first_divergence.description: seq index of first mismatch. Null on pass.

error.description: Populated when status is one of: failed, timeout, build_error, infra_error. Null otherwise.

phase.description: Phase in which the error occurred.

recoverable.description: Whether the orchestrator should auto-retry this run.

repro_cmd.description: CLI command to reproduce the failure from scratch.

retry_policy.description: Orchestrator retry configuration for infra-level failures (OOM, DISK_FULL, ISS_CRASH).

retry_count.description: Number of retries attempted so far.

retry_on_timeout.description: Whether to retry when DUT or ISS times out. Usually false — timeouts often indicate bugs.

backoff_s.description: Seconds to wait before each retry.

environment.description: Host environment snapshot captured at run creation time for full reproducibility.

os.description: OS name and version, e.g. 'Ubuntu 22.04.3 LTS'.

git_commit.description: AVA repo git commit hash at time of run.

git_dirty.description: True if repo had uncommitted changes.

metrics.description: Run performance and quality metrics. Populated by the orchestrator after all phases complete.

tags.description: Free-form labels for regression bucketing and dashboard filtering. Convention: 'ext:M', 'bug:div_by_zero', 'type:random', 'suite:compliance', 'priority:p0'.

notifications.description: Optional post-run callback configuration for CI/CD integration.

on_events.description: Which terminal states trigger a notification.

completed_at.description: Set by orchestrator when status reaches a terminal state.
